# Copilot History of Prompts

## Files I wrote (or copied from elsewhere - not produced by AI)

- .github/copilot-instructions.md
- .github/prompts/proposal.prompt.md
- .github/prompts/spec.prompt.md

## Prompt List

I have an idea for an application called ProPulse. ProPulse is a web-based microservices system for blogging and social media marketing. Authors can publish articles on the site; readers can read, rate, and comment on articles. An enterprise social media marketing manager can schedule articles to be published on various social media sites. Create a product proposal for the ProPulse application in docs/product-proposal.md.
[ proposal.prompt.md; Agent; Claude 3.7 Sonnet (Preview) ]

I like this proposal. Let's split the frontend into two parts: The reading interface will be used by the majority of users and provide reading, search, comments, and ratings. This will use a combination of Razor Pages and JavaScript/CSS to produce the UI. The publishing interface will be used by the authors and social media managers and provide article editing, scheduling of social media posts, and views into the analytics data.

<-- MARKS COMPLETION OF product-proposal.md; Approx time to complete: 30 minutes -->

I think this is good now. Let's move onto the technical specification. Technical specifications will be built based on the phased approach that you already suggested. Let's create the phase-1 technical specification in docs/phase1/technical-specification.md. Based on your understanding of the product capabilities and required architecture, create the phase-1 technical specification.
[ spec.prompt.md; Agent; Claude 3.7 Sonnet (Preview) ]

The identity service should be based on ASP.NET Identity and use OAuth 2.0 and OIDC for communication, allowing both a standardized mechanism for storing identities as well as integrating with social authentication schemes.  In this scheme, the various front-end services will redirect to the identity service to authenticate users, which gets a JWT that can then be used as an authorization source to the backend microservice APIs.  I do like the idea of including a UserProfile, but it can be stored as an extension of the IdentityUser model.

Replace the FirstName and LastName with a DisplayName.  Do not include a UserType - this will be handle by the IdentityRole from ASP.NET Identity.  The CreatedDate should be renamed CreatedAt and be a DateTimeOffset, automatically managed by the database service via a trigger.  The LastLoginDate should be renamed LastLoggedInAt and be a DateTimeOffset?  The SocialMediaHandles should be moved to a separate table and linked via a 1:many relationship.   Add a sub-section that describes the relationships between models and provides and entity relationship diagram (erDiagram) using mermaid.

Yes, please make similar updates to the other service models to ensure consistency.  Also, ensure all Ids are represented as strings for consistency.  Finally, as a UpdatedAt property as a database managed DateTimeOffset to all entities.

Convert all the entity description lists into GFM formatted tables with columns "Column Name", "Type", "Description", "Example" for readability.

Each microservice that exposes a web API should also expose an OpenAPI document at /openapi/index.json.  The API gateway should expose a composite document based on the exposed APIs.  In development, use Scalar to allow the developer to test the API configuration.

While generating the last update, you removed sections 6+ that covered DevOps, Security, etc. Can you please put those back?

In section 7.6, please update to ensure Blazor WebAssembly is used.

In section 8.1, change the database schema management so that it uses a separate DbUp based project for database management. We'll arrange to run the DbUp project during database startup phase of the Aspire project.

Take a look at all the Web APIs.  They should all support RFC 9110 style conditional requests.  Primarily, this means returning a Last-Modified and ETag header, and supporting If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since headers when doing individual entity operations.  The entities will need to be updated to include a row version that can be converted into the ETag.  Since we use both PostgreSQL and SQLite, a trigger should be used to generate the row version rather than relying on the xmin property for PostgreSQL.

Let's work on some final editrs. Firstly, in section 9, I'm using .NET Aspire. This automatically builds containers for me within the production interface. As a result, I don't need docker or docker-compose configuration. I will need a differential Aspire configuration that uses an Azure PostgreSQL in Production and Staging, but a container in Development. When in Azure, I'll use managed identity to secure the connection between the various microservices hosted in ACA and the postgresql database.

Nope. Let's take a look at the proposed entity type configurations. I don't think the cascade delete semantics are going to work because the models and database tables are per-microservice. When a user is deleted, all content (articles, ratings, comments, etc.) associated with that user need to be deleted. When an article is deleted, all content (attachments, ratings, comments, etc.) associated with the article need to be deleted. This cascades throughout the system. Categories and Tags are really the only features immune from this cascade delete (although the association between article and tag/category will be removed when an article is deleted). While this can be managed within a single database, please discuss how it will be managed in this case. We can defer data deletion to a later phase of the MVP if necessary.

-- RESPONSE --

You've raised a critical architectural challenge regarding cascade delete semantics in our microservice architecture. Since each microservice has its own database, traditional SQL CASCADE DELETE operations won't work across service boundaries, yet we need to maintain data consistency when deleting entities like users or articles that have relationships across multiple services.

Let me address this challenge and propose a comprehensive solution:

The Cross-Service Cascading Delete Challenge
Current State
Identity Service manages users
Content Service manages articles, categories, and tags
Community Service manages comments and ratings
Distribution Service manages social media shares
When a user or article is deleted, related data across these services must be handled properly to maintain consistency.

Proposed Solution: Event-Driven Deletion with Soft Delete Pattern
I recommend implementing a hybrid approach that combines immediate soft deletion with eventual hard deletion through an event-driven architecture:
* It went on to propose a solution.

-- ENDRESPONSE --

Would the event coordination proposed in phase 1 require additional services? If so, what services are required? I'm assuming some sort of message bus?

I'd like to also continue the development paradigm where everything is runnable locally using Aspire. Is there a containerized message broker that I can use instead of Azure Service Bus when running in development mode?

Great. Let's add those details to the technical specifications. Also, update the product-proposal to indicate that the hard delete service will be added in phase 2.

[ At this point, I'm happy with docs/phase1/technical-specification.md; total time = 4 hours; I'm going to do a final pass on the technical specification with deep thinking tomorrow morning ]
